name: Macademia-Orange Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. In a bowl, beat together the butter, sugar, and vanilla until light and creamy.
3. Add the flour and macadamia nuts; mix until combined.
4. Chill the dough in the freezer for 10 minutes.
5. Roll the chilled dough into ¾-inch-diameter balls and place them about 2 inches apart on a parchment-covered baking sheet.
6. Flatten slightly with your hands. Bake until just beginning to brown, about 15 minutes. Remove from the oven and let cool on a wire rack.
7. For candied orange rind:
- Cut 2 oranges in half across their width and scoop out the flesh (save for another use).
- Slice the rinds into 1/8- to ¼-inch-thick strips.
- Place the strips in a saucepan with 4 cups of water and bring to a boil over high heat.
- Boil for 25 minutes, then drain.
8. In a separate pan, mix the remaining sugar and about 1 ¾ cups of the boiling water. Simmer over medium-low heat for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally until thickened.
9. Strain the syrup through a fine-mesh sieve to remove strips; return rinds to the saucepan with some syrup.
10. Melt white chocolate according to package instructions.
11. Dip the bottom of each cooled cookie into the melted chocolate, coating about ¼ inch up the side.
12. Place the dipped cookies onto parchment paper lined baking sheets.
13. Press a strip of candied orange rind around the bottom of each cookie where it adheres to the chocolate.
14. Allow the cookies to set before serving within a few hours.
